(A) pleasure exists for its own sake.
(B) pleasure exists for the sake of operation.
(C) operation exists for the sake of pleasure.
(D) both b and c.
The correct answer is (B) pleasure exists for the sake of operation..

Anscombe claims that the notion of moral obligation is derived from the concept of:

(A) preference.
(B) maximizing utility.
(C) positive law.
(D) divine law.
The correct answer is (D) divine law..

In Brandt’s terminology, a moral code has currency in a society only if:

(A) a high proportion of the adults in the society subscribe to its principles.
(B) it is recognized as the moral code of the society.
(C) both a and b.
(D) neither a nor b.
The correct answer is (C) both a and b..

The morality that Gauthier describes as facing a foundational crisis essentially involves:

(A) enlightened egoism.
(B) pure altruism.
(C) justified constraint.
(D) prima facie duties.
The correct answer is (C) justified constraint..

In spite of the fact that Socrates claims to be ignorant of the essence or nature of certain things like justice, he is wise insofar as he recognizes that without such knowledge actions are rationally unjustified.  That is, his wisdom consists in his recognition not only that he is ignorant of such essences but also that:

(A) justice, like knowledge, requires that we admit that we know nothing and never will.
(B) he knows what he is supposed to be looking for--knowledge of the essences of things.
(C) knowledge of the essences of things is impossible, because that would require that we know what we are looking for before we know what it is we are looking for.
(D) his method of asking questions about essences is itself unjustified because he does not know why he engages in such a practice.
The correct answer is (B) he knows what he is supposed to be looking for--knowledge of the essences of things..

Hare refers to people who endorse ideals without regard for whether people’s interests are pursued by them as:

(A) fanatics.
(B) amoralists.
(C) immoralists.
(D) maniacs.
The correct answer is (A) fanatics..

In Aquinas’s view, acts of prudence are solely about matters of:

(A) pleasure.
(B) desire.
(C) moral virtue.
(D) piety.
The correct answer is (C) moral virtue..

According to Locke, a man in the state of nature will relinquish his absolute freedom to the state because _____.

(A) he will also enjoy absolute freedom when subject to the state
(B) in the state of nature the enjoyment of his freedom is very uncertain and vulnerable
(C) he wants to have absolute power over others
(D) he rejects the laws of the state of nature
The correct answer is (B) in the state of nature the enjoyment of his freedom is very uncertain and vulnerable.

Augustine claims that there can be no evil where there is no:

(A) hatred.
(B) greed.
(C) love.
(D) good.
The correct answer is (D) good..

According to Ross, justice is:

(A) when all laws are followed.
(B) when contracts and promises are adhered to.
(C) when the distribution of happiness is in accordance with merit.
(D) when the guilty are punished for their crimes.
The correct answer is (C) when the distribution of happiness is in accordance with merit..

Logical behaviorism is at odds with our commonsense intuition that mental states _____.

(A) cause behavior
(B) never cause behavior
(C) exist
(D) do not exist
The correct answer is (A) cause behavior.

Ross claims that we learn of our prima facie duties:

(A) by seeing the prima facie rightness of particular acts, and then apprehending general principles.
(B) by apprehending general principles, and then inferring the prima facie rightness of particular acts.
(C) by proving them philosophically.
(D) from the explicit moral instruction we receive as children.
The correct answer is (A) by seeing the prima facie rightness of particular acts, and then apprehending general principles..

Anscombe criticizes as absurd Kant’s idea of:

(A) the thing in itself.
(B) the categorical imperative.
(C) the phenomenal self.
(D) legislating for oneself.
The correct answer is (D) legislating for oneself..

In the Inquiry, Hume claims that our final verdicts on moral matters are derived from:

(A) reason.
(B) a moral sense that is unique to each person.
(C) a moral sense that is universal in our species.
(D) emotion.
The correct answer is (C) a moral sense that is universal in our species..

According to Nagel, the problem of moral luck arises because:

(A) philosophers try to apply arbitrarily stringent standards to moral responsibility.
(B) the self is threatened with dissolution by the absorption of its acts into the class of events.
(C) our moral theories are hopelessly off track.
(D) our theories of human action are hopelessly off track.
The correct answer is (B) the self is threatened with dissolution by the absorption of its acts into the class of events..

A moral theory explains _____.

(A) why an action is right or wrong
(B) why one moral event caused another
(C) where a moral agent got her values
(D) why people do what they do
The correct answer is (A) why an action is right or wrong.

Anscombe criticizes Butler on the grounds that:

(A) our consciences are fallible.
(B) the notion of “legislating for oneself” is absurd.
(C) we really can derive an “ought” from an “is.”
(D) the concept of “pleasure” is unclear.
The correct answer is (A) our consciences are fallible..

Mill claims that a happy life is one of:

(A) tranquility.
(B) excitement.
(C) both a and b.
(D) neither a nor b.
The correct answer is (C) both a and b..

According to Moore, “pleasure is good” means the same thing as:

(A) “pleasure is desired.”
(B) “pleasure is pleasant.”
(C) “we desire to desire pleasure.”
(D) none of the above.
The correct answer is (D) none of the above..

To argue that he is obliged to obey the laws of Athens, Socrates compares the city to:

(A) an employer.
(B) a parent.
(C) a friend.
(D) a spouse.
The correct answer is (B) a parent..

Soft determinism entails that _____.

(A) we are free to choose our desires
(B) there are no restraints on human behavior
(C) the thesis of determinism is false
(D) the thesis of determinism is true
The correct answer is (D) the thesis of determinism is true.

In the moral life, feelings are _____.

(A) essential and inevitable
(B) essential to impartiality
(C) an unerring guide
(D) reason based
The correct answer is (A) essential and inevitable.

According to Cicero, it is expedient to be:

(A) just.
(B) generous.
(C) kind.
(D) all of the above.
The correct answer is (D) all of the above..

Brandt claims that whether a moral code is ideal depends in part on:

(A) the complexity of the rules.
(B) how many people would likely violate it.
(C) the costs of its restrictions, in terms of the guilt it induces.
(D) all of the above.
The correct answer is (D) all of the above..

The view that we consist of two distinct substances (body and mind) and that these two interact is known as _____.

(A) functionalism
(B) identity theory
(C) substance dualism
(D) materialism
The correct answer is (C) substance dualism.

According to Stevenson, the word “good” has a pleasing emotive meaning that fits it for:

(A) descriptive use.
(B) constructive use.
(C) dynamic use.
(D) propositional use.
The correct answer is (C) dynamic use..

According to Locke, the chief end of men's uniting into a commonwealth is _____.

(A) domination over other commonwealths
(B) the preservation of the state of nature
(C) the preservation of their property
(D) the preservation of their absolute power
The correct answer is (C) the preservation of their property.

Hare claims that once two people agree on the meaning of the term “ought”:

(A) they will share all the same moral opinions.
(B) they will disagree morally only if they disagree about the facts of the case.
(C) they will disagree morally only if they have different inclinations.
(D) none of the above.
The correct answer is (D) none of the above..

To explain the notion of a prima facie duty, Ross draws an analogy with:

(A) natural laws.
(B) the laws of a nation.
(C) the commands of a dictator.
(D) our emotions.
The correct answer is (A) natural laws..

In the Republic, Thrasymachus claims that justice is:

(A) telling the truth and paying one’s debts.
(B) doing what pleases the gods.
(C) the advantage of the stronger.
(D) a harmony of the soul.
The correct answer is (C) the advantage of the stronger..

Epictetus claims that the desire for one’s loved ones to live forever is:

(A) a natural instinct.
(B) a sign of genuine love.
(C) blasphemous.
(D) silly.
The correct answer is (D) silly..

According to Sartre, the first principle of existentialism is that _____.

(A) God is dead
(B) man is all-powerful
(C) man is nothing else but what he makes of himself
(D) man is nothing
The correct answer is (C) man is nothing else but what he makes of himself.

Aristotle claims that:

(A) virtue is in our power, and so is vice.
(B) virtue is in our power, but vice is not.
(C) vice is in our power, but virtue is not.
(D) neither virtue nor vice is in our power.
The correct answer is (A) virtue is in our power, and so is vice..

Hume describes reason as:

(A) cool and disengaged.
(B) the source of all moral actions.
(C) the first spring or impulse to desire.
(D) all of the above.
The correct answer is (A) cool and disengaged..

According to Brandt, John Stuart Mill’s view most closely resembles:

(A) act-utilitarianism.
(B) a rule-utilitarianism based on the actual rules of society.
(C) a rule-utilitarianism based on ideal moral rules.
(D) Kant’s ethics.
The correct answer is (C) a rule-utilitarianism based on ideal moral rules..

The version of utilitarianism that Brandt defends makes rightness a function of:

(A) the consequences of particular acts.
(B) the actual and recognized rules of society.
(C) ideal rules.
(D) whether or not one’s maxim can be universalized.
The correct answer is (C) ideal rules..

"When a person starts on the discovery of the absolute by the light of reason only, and without any assistance of sense, and perseveres until by pure intelligence he arrives at the perception of the absolute good, he at last finds himself at the end of the intellectual world. . . . Dialectic, and dialectic alone, goes directly to the first principle and is the only science which does away with hypotheses in order to make her ground secure."  Here Plato indicates how hypothetical knowledge cannot provide the foundation of dialectical knowledge, insofar as hypotheses simply:

(A) explain sense experiences in terms of general concepts which themselves are not explained.
(B) show how particular objects of experience cause us to recall innate ideas.
(C) describe sense experience without providing an explanation for dialectical methods.
(D) reject the use of reason, preferring instead dialectic, to achieve knowledge.
The correct answer is (A) explain sense experiences in terms of general concepts which themselves are not explained..

What are some of the frequent frustrations in writing or reading about research ethics?

(A) Writers differ over what is ethically acceptable.
(B) The same debates are rehearsed over decades.
(C) Cases of ethical violation tend to be linked with certain research methods.
(D) All of the above.
The correct answer is (D) All of the above..

According to Cicero, immorality is __________, and expediency is __________.

(A) repugnant to Nature; repugnant to Nature.
(B) repugnant to Nature; in accord with Nature.
(C) in accord with Nature; repugnant to Nature.
(D) in accord with Nature; in accord with Nature.
The correct answer is (B) repugnant to Nature; in accord with Nature..

Epicurus conceives of death as:

(A) the beginning of the afterlife.
(B) one component of the continuous cycle of rebirth.
(C) the deprivation of all sensation.
(D) the greatest harm that one can suffer.
The correct answer is (C) the deprivation of all sensation..

Nussbaum claims that at the first stage of ethical inquiry, terms for the virtues should be:

(A) as descriptive as possible.
(B) those that are conventionally used.
(C) neutral between competing specifications of the virtue.
(D) none of the above.
The correct answer is (C) neutral between competing specifications of the virtue..

The four main divisions of philosophy are metaphysics, epistemology, axiology, and _____.

(A) bioethics
(B) logic
(C) aesthetics
(D) categorical logic
The correct answer is (B) logic.

Da Vinci's Mona Lisa is_____.

(A) representational
(B) abstract
(C) obscure
(D) nonrepresentational
The correct answer is (A) representational.

According to Butler, the degree to which one acts compassionately depends on:

(A) the strength of his affection of compassion.
(B) the strength of his other affections besides compassion.
(C) both a and b.
(D) neither a nor b.
The correct answer is (C) both a and b..

One example of bad constitutive moral luck would be:

(A) a person who is by nature envious and ungenerous.
(B) a person who lived in Nazi Germany in 1940.
(C) a person who drives drunk and hits a child.
(D) all of the above.
The correct answer is (A) a person who is by nature envious and ungenerous..

Moore defines the naturalistic fallacy as the view that:

(A) everything that is natural is good.
(B) everything that is good is natural.
(C) good is identical to a natural property.
(D) all of the above.
The correct answer is (C) good is identical to a natural property..

Mill claims that the principle of utility:

(A) can be proven from self-evident principles.
(B) can be proven from principles that are known empirically.
(C) cannot be proven, and this is a unique problem for the theory.
(D) cannot be proven, but this is common to all first principles.
The correct answer is (D) cannot be proven, but this is common to all first principles..

The theory that says mental states are nothing but brain states is known as _____.

(A) brain theory
(B) epiphenominalism
(C) identity theory
(D) logical behaviorism
The correct answer is (C) identity theory.

Butler claims that in the strictest and most proper sense, tofollow nature is to:

(A) act as one pleases.
(B) act on any of one’s passions.
(C) act on one’s strongest passion.
(D) act on one’s conscience.
The correct answer is (D) act on one’s conscience..

According to Craig, the kalam cosmological argument establishes that _____.

(A) the God of Christianity exists
(B) the universe has a cause
(C) the Big Bang model is false
(D) the universe is uncaused
The correct answer is (B) the universe has a cause.

Singer claims that famine relief organizations direct our aid to distant refugees:

(A) more effectively than we could get it to our close neighbors.
(B) almost as effectively as we could get it to our close neighbors.
(C) quite inefficiently, but they are still worth donating to.
(D) very inefficiently, and are therefore not worth donating to.
The correct answer is (B) almost as effectively as we could get it to our close neighbors..

Kant says that when trying to decide whether an action is morally permissible, we must ask if we can consistently will that the maxim of our action should become _____.

(A) a rule for maximizing happiness
(B) a contingent law
(C) a universal law
(D) a rule of thumb
The correct answer is (C) a universal law.

Aesthetic values have to do with _______

(A) the moral value of works of art and other objects that could be judged beautiful
(B) good and bad works of art and other objects that could be judged beautiful
(C) the moral values of artists and critics
(D) pragmatic decisions regarding the display of art
The correct answer is (B) good and bad works of art and other objects that could be judged beautiful.

According to Feinberg, a good moral education:

(A) will make no use of pleasure or pain as sanctions.
(B) will be based entirely on the use of pleasure and pain as sanctions.
(C) will produce someone who does the right thing out of deference to authority.
(D) will produce someone who does the right thing simply because it is right.
The correct answer is (D) will produce someone who does the right thing simply because it is right..

For Socrates, the belief that "virtue is knowledge" is related to his claim that "the unexamined life is not worth living," because he believes that:

(A) the unexamined life is one in which we live day to day without asking questions about who we are and why we are here in the first place.
(B) the Delphic oracle identified Socrates as the wisest person on earth because he claimed to know nothing.
(C) by questioning traditional beliefs, we learn to recognize how some answers seem to be more satisfactory than others.
(D) the only way to be a good or worthwhile person is to know how human beings should behave based on universal norms or values.
The correct answer is (D) the only way to be a good or worthwhile person is to know how human beings should behave based on universal norms or values..

Craig says an actually infinite number of things _____.

(A) is possible
(B) is meaningless
(C) exists somewhere
(D) cannot exist
The correct answer is (D) cannot exist.

Descartes believed that interaction between body and mind took place in _____.

(A) ectoplasm
(B) the brain stem
(C) the pineal gland
(D) the nervous system
The correct answer is (C) the pineal gland.

Which of the following is a form of harm that might be suffered by research participants?

(A) Physical injury
(B) Stress and anxiety
(C) Impaired development
(D) All of the above
The correct answer is (D) All of the above.

One of the aims of philosophy is to think critically about whether there are good reasons for adopting our beliefs.  Reasons are considered "good reasons" if they are consistent with everyday experience and:

(A) are part of a set of religious, moral, or political beliefs that an individual feels deeply about.
(B) are considered good by at least one culture, sub-culture, or individual.
(C) cannot be interpreted in different ways by different people or cultures.
(D) take into account objections, are acceptable to impartial third parties, and avoid undesirable consequences.
The correct answer is (D) take into account objections, are acceptable to impartial third parties, and avoid undesirable consequences..

The existence of a form of mental illness known as multiple personality disorder seems to suggest that _____.

(A) the existence of the mind is a delusion
(B) the mind is divisible
(C) the mind is indivisible
(D) mental illness is incurable
The correct answer is (B) the mind is divisible.

Singer’s argument begins with the assumption that:

(A) all people are created equal.
(B) we ought to do whatever maximizes happiness.
(C) pleasure is good.
(D) suffering and death from lack of food, shelter, and medical care are bad.
The correct answer is (D) suffering and death from lack of food, shelter, and medical care are bad..

Interest theories of the good define goodness in terms of:

(A) approval or desire.
(B) benefit to oneself.
(C) the consequences of one’s actions.
(D) prima facie duties.
The correct answer is (A) approval or desire..

Brandt views his rule-utilitarianism as a:

(A) normative ethical thesis.
(B) metaethical thesis.
(C) purely descriptive thesis.
(D) both a and b.
The correct answer is (A) normative ethical thesis..

Parfit claims that choosing to save the larger number in a trade-off case:

(A) does not reflect an equal concern for the survival of each, for it does not give equal weight to saving each.
(B) does not reflect an equal concern for the survival of each, for it does not give each an equal chance of survival.
(C) reflects an equal concern for the survival of each, for it gives equal weight to saving each.
(D) reflects an equal concern for the survival of each, for it gives each an equal chance of survival.
The correct answer is (C) reflects an equal concern for the survival of each, for it gives equal weight to saving each..

In Plato's idealism, the unchanging Ideas or "Forms" in terms of which sensible objects both exist and are known must transcend (that is, exist beyond) the changing realm of appearances; because if Forms changed, then:

(A) the only things in the sensible world that we could ever experience would be concepts.
(B) the sensible realm (in contrast to the intelligible realm) would consist only of copies of real things.
(C) nothing in the experienced world could be or be identified as one determinate thing or another.
(D) the sensible world would consist of unchanging Forms.
The correct answer is (C) nothing in the experienced world could be or be identified as one determinate thing or another..

Epictetus claims that the most important part of philosophy deals with:

(A) which principles of action are correct.
(B) the explanation for why certain principles of action are correct.
(C) the explanation of why certain demonstrations are valid.
(D) none of the above.
The correct answer is (A) which principles of action are correct..

According to Locke, the legislative power is limited by _____.

(A) the impartial rule of established laws
(B) different rules for rich and poor
(C) laws established for the good of the state
(D) a monarch
The correct answer is (A) the impartial rule of established laws.

According to James, indeterminism allows that the world has _____.

(A) no ambiguous possibilities
(B) a fixed future
(C) ambiguous possibilities
(D) no shadow of turning
The correct answer is (C) ambiguous possibilities.

The view that we ought to relieve the agony of one stranger rather than the minor pain of another is endorsed by:

(A) Taurek and Parfit.
(B) Taurek, but not Parfit.
(C) Parfit, but not Taurek.
(D) neither Parfit nor Taurek.
The correct answer is (C) Parfit, but not Taurek..

Aquinas argues that every agent acts for the sake of:

(A) pleasure.
(B) self-interest.
(C) pleasing God.
(D) an end.
The correct answer is (D) an end..

Descartes reasons that the very fact that he is thinking shows that _____.

(A) he does not exist
(B) he is not being deceived
(C) he exists
(D) he is dreaming
The correct answer is (C) he exists.

The philosophic insistence on providing a logos for the world and our experience of it might itself rely ultimately on adopting a certain mythos, insofar as:

(A) philosophy assumes that it is possible and meaningful to reason about the world and experience.
(B) the myths of philosophy are really lies that are told to make so-called philosophic enquiries sound more respectable.
(C) philosophy is based on logic, whereas myths are not based on logic.
(D) mythos refers to the philosophic understanding of the world, whereas logos refers to the philosophic understanding of our experience of the world.
The correct answer is (A) philosophy assumes that it is possible and meaningful to reason about the world and experience..

Wolf defines a “moral saint” as a person who:

(A) is as morally worthy as possible.
(B) is as religiously observant as possible.
(C) dies for the cause of morality.
(D) all of the above.
The correct answer is (A) is as morally worthy as possible..

According to Epicurus:

(A) every pleasure is good, and every pleasure is to be chosen.
(B) every pleasure is good, but not every pleasure is to be chosen.
(C) not every pleasure is good, but every pleasure is to be chosen.
(D) not every pleasure is good, and not every pleasure is to be chosen.
The correct answer is (B) every pleasure is good, but not every pleasure is to be chosen..

According to Kant, the basis of morality is the concept of:

(A) charity.
(B) fairness.
(C) piety.
(D) freedom.
The correct answer is (D) freedom..

According to Taylor, hard determinism conflicts with _____.

(A) the findings of science
(B) the fact of deliberation and our sense that some actions are up to us
(C) the fact of deliberation and indeterminism
(D) scientific determinism
The correct answer is (B) the fact of deliberation and our sense that some actions are up to us.

Descartes argues against trusting the senses on the grounds that _____.

(A) they never directly deceive him
(B) they sometimes deceive him
(C) God allows sensory deception
(D) sense perception is indubitable
The correct answer is (B) they sometimes deceive him.

A question-and-answer dialogue in which propositions are methodically scrutinized to uncover the truth is known as _____.

(A) an argument
(B) the Socratic method
(C) the Socratic jest
(D) a debate
The correct answer is (B) the Socratic method.

According to Bentham, the principle of utility is appropriately applied to:

(A) individuals.
(B) governments.
(C) both a and b.
(D) neither a nor b.
The correct answer is (C) both a and b..

Augustine claims that to save another person from injury, it is permissible to:

(A) lie.
(B) steal.
(C) both a and b.
(D) neither a nor. b.
The correct answer is (D) neither a nor. b..

The purpose of the veil of ignorance is to:

(A) prevent contractors from adopting principles of justice that unfairly advantage themselves.
(B) make the decision of which principles of justice to adopt simpler and easier.
(C) ensure that the principles of justice selected are not too complicated for ordinary people to follow.
(D) all of the above.
The correct answer is (A) prevent contractors from adopting principles of justice that unfairly advantage themselves..

Butler argues that the belief that there is a conflict between public goods and private goods arises from our notions of:

(A) justice.
(B) benevolence.
(C) property.
(D) charity.
The correct answer is (C) property..

Socrates suggests that the holy is one part of:

(A) what is prudent.
(B) what is just.
(C) what is beautiful.
(D) what is legal.
The correct answer is (B) what is just..

Baggini says that almost all deniers of meaning in life really seem to be rejecting only the idea that life has _____.

(A) internal meaning
(B) external meaning
(C) religious meaning
(D) secular meaning
The correct answer is (B) external meaning.

According to Nagel, the view that moral luck is paradoxical is:

(A) a logical mistake.
(B) an ethical mistake.
(C) a factual mistake.
(D) not a mistake.
The correct answer is (D) not a mistake..

According to Nagel, if a driver hits a child through no fault of his own:

(A) he will feel terrible about the event.
(B) he will reproach himself.
(C) he will have been the victim of moral bad luck.
(D) all of the above.
The correct answer is (A) he will feel terrible about the event..

Questions like “What is knowledge?” and “What is truth?” are mainstays in the branch of philosophy known as _____.

(A) logic
(B) metaphysics
(C) epistemology
(D) aesthetics
The correct answer is (C) epistemology.

According to Hobbes, in the state of nature, notions of right and wrong or justice and injustice _____.

(A) have no place
(B) refer to objective standards
(C) still apply to the actions of men
(D) serve as guiding ideals
The correct answer is (A) have no place.

Paley says that every indication of contrivance and design that exists in the watch exists in _____.

(A) God
(B) infinity
(C) the works of nature
(D) time
The correct answer is (C) the works of nature.

Which of the following would Gauthier not accept?

(A) Morality faces a foundational crisis.
(B) Deliberative justification is incomplete.
(C) In some situations, everyone will do better if they mutually agree to constrain their pursuit of self-interest.
(D) A rational agent is one who acts to achieve the maximal fulfillment of her preferences.
The correct answer is (B) Deliberative justification is incomplete..

One of the tasks of philosophy is to test conceptual frameworks for depth and consistency.  It does this through (1) expressing our ideas in clear, concise language and (2) supporting those ideas with reasons and with overcoming objections to them.  Philosophy thus emphasizes the need to:

(A) pose questions that can be resolved not by reasoning but only by faith or personal belief.
(B) show why the beliefs adopted by most people in a culture are preferable since more people understand those beliefs and see no reason to raise objections to them.
(C) articulate what we mean by our beliefs and to justify our beliefs by arguments.
(D) develop a set of ideas about the nature of society (i.e., an ideology) that can be used to support a religious conceptual framework.
The correct answer is (C) articulate what we mean by our beliefs and to justify our beliefs by arguments..

Brandt claims that act-utilitarianism:

(A) has implausible consequences.
(B) gives rise to moral dilemmas.
(C) is self-contradictory.
(D) all of the above.
The correct answer is (A) has implausible consequences..

Psychological egoism is:

(A) an ethical theory about how we ought to behave.
(B) a generalization concerning the way people tend to behave.
(C) a claim about human nature and the ways people are capable of behaving.
(D) none of the above.
The correct answer is (C) a claim about human nature and the ways people are capable of behaving..

According to Moore’s “ideal utilitarianism,” the right action is the one that brings about the greatest amount of:

(A) pleasure.
(B) happiness.
(C) good.
(D) virtue.
The correct answer is (C) good..

According to d'Holbach, people always act according to _____.

(A) free choices
(B) dictates of the soul
(C) necessary natural laws
(D) undetermined will
The correct answer is (C) necessary natural laws.

Before Tolstoy's Christian conversion, what was his perspective on the meaning of life?

(A) optimist
(B) satisfied
(C) nominally religious
(D) pessimist
The correct answer is (D) pessimist.

The study of reality in the broadest sense, an inquiry into the elemental nature of the universe and the things in it, is known as _____.

(A) metaphysics
(B) epistemology
(C) quantum physics
(D) axiology
The correct answer is (A) metaphysics.

Identify the conclusion of the following argument. It is hard not to verify in our peers the same weakened intelligence due to emotions that we observe in our everyday patients. The arrogance of our consciousness, which in general, belongs to the strongest defense mechanisms, blocks the unconscious complexes. Because of this, it is difficult to convince people of the unconscious, and in turn to teach them what their conscious knowledge contradicts. (Sigmund Freud, The Origin and Development of Psychoanalysis)

(A) It is hard not to verify in our peers the same weakened intelligence due to emotions that we observe in our everyday patients.
(B) The arrogance of our consciousness, which in general, belongs to the strongest defense mechanisms, blocks the unconscious complexes.
(C) Because of this, it is difficult to convince people of the unconscious, and in turn to teach them what their conscious knowledge contradicts.
(D) It is difficult to convince people of the unconscious, and in turn to teach them what their conscious knowledge contradicts.
The correct answer is (D) It is difficult to convince people of the unconscious, and in turn to teach them what their conscious knowledge contradicts..

Select the best translation into predicate logic. David teaches Chris. (c: Chris; d: David; Txy: x teaches y)

The correct answer is (A) Tdc.

(B) Some houses are bigger than all large apartments.

(B) Consistent. Consistent valuation when P and Q are true and R and S are false
(C) Consistent. Consistent valuation when P, Q, R, and S are true
(D) Consistent. Consistent valuation when R and S are true and P and Q are false
The correct answer is (A) Inconsistent.

Use indirect truth tables to determine whether the following argument is valid. If the argument is invalid, choose an option which presents a counterexample. (There may be other counterexamples as well.)
A ⊃ (~B ≡ C)
B ≡ D
~C ≡ ~D / ~A

(C) Invalid. Counterexample when A and B are true and C and D are false
(D) Invalid. Counterexample when A, C, and D are true and B is false
The correct answer is (A) Valid.

Construct a complete truth table for the following pairs of propositions. Then, using the truth tables, determine whether the statements are logically equivalent or contradictory. If neither, determine whether they are consistent or inconsistent. Justify your answers.
A ⊃ ~B and B ⊃ A

(A) Logically equivalent
(B) Contradictory
(C) Neither logically equivalent nor contradictory, but consistent
(D) Inconsistent
The correct answer is (C) Neither logically equivalent nor contradictory, but consistent.

Use indirect truth tables to determine whether each set of propositions is consistent. If the set is consistent, choose an option with a consistent valuation. (There may be other consistent valuations.)
(G ≡ H) ⊃ H
~H ∨ I
G · ~I

(B) Consistent. Consistent valuation when G, H, and I are true
(C) Consistent. Consistent valuation when G and H are true and I is false
(D) Consistent. Consistent valuation when G is true and H and I are false
The correct answer is (D) Consistent. Consistent valuation when G is true and H and I are false.

Select the best translation into predicate logic: No people drive on Mars.

(A) ~Pd
(B) (∀x)(Px ∨ ~Dx)
(C) (∀x)(Px ⊃ ~Dx)

Select the best translation into predicate logic.George borrows Hector's lawnmower. (g: George; h: Hector; l: Hector's lawnmower; Bxyx: x borrows y from z)

(A) Blgh
(B) Bhlg
(C) Bglh

Select the best English interpretation of the given arguments in predicate logic.
Dm
